incorrects.description: Student selected 2 × 4: reversed the groups and items per group

params.description: All 4 circles highlight to show the group count.

steps.text: Count the circles first. That's your groups. How many circles do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 + 2: confused addition with multiplication

params.description: One circle highlights to show equal groups structure.

steps.text: When you have equal groups, you multiply. The × symbol shows groups of the same size. How many circles? How many dots in each?

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 + 4: confused addition with multiplication and reversed values

params.description: All circles highlight to show the equal groups structure.

steps.text: Equal groups means multiply, not add. Count the circles. That's the first number. Count the dots in one circle. That's the second number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Circles highlight one by one as they are counted: 1, 2, 3, 4. Then one circle opens to reveal its 2 dots, which highlight: 1, 2.

steps.text: Let me show you. First, count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four circles. Those are my groups. Now look inside one circle: 1, 2. Two dots in each. So I have 4 groups of 2. That's 4 × 2. The first number is always the groups. The second is how many in each group.
